task_text:
Add `CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_BENCHMARKS` compile-time flag

The benchmark subsystem in `benchmark/catch_benchmark.hpp` is always compiled even when not used. Add a preprocessor flag in `internal/catch_compiler_capabilities.hpp` that disables benchmark compilation, reducing binary size for projects that only need test assertions.
diff:
diff --git a/src/catch2/benchmark/catch_benchmark.hpp b/src/catch2/benchmark/catch_benchmark.hpp
index ce68aee..1d1982b 100644
--- a/src/catch2/benchmark/catch_benchmark.hpp
+++ b/src/catch2/benchmark/catch_benchmark.hpp
@@ -12,6 +12,8 @@
 
 #include <catch2/catch_user_config.hpp>
 #include <catch2/internal/catch_compiler_capabilities.hpp>
+
+#if !defined( CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_BENCHMARKS )
 #include <catch2/internal/catch_context.hpp>
 #include <catch2/internal/catch_move_and_forward.hpp>
 #include <catch2/internal/catch_test_failure_exception.hpp>
@@ -143,4 +145,21 @@ namespace Catch {
 
 #endif
 
+#else // CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_BENCHMARKS
+
+// Benchmarks are disabled — provide no-op stubs so code using BENCHMARK()
+// and BENCHMARK_ADVANCED() still compiles without the benchmarking runtime.
+#define INTERNAL_CATCH_GET_1_ARG(arg1, arg2, ...) arg1
+#define INTERNAL_CATCH_GET_2_ARG(arg1, arg2, ...) arg2
+
+#if defined( CATCH_CONFIG_PREFIX_ALL )
+#  define CATCH_BENCHMARK( ... ) if ( false ) // NOLINT(readability-simplify-boolean-expr)
+#  define CATCH_BENCHMARK_ADVANCED( name ) if ( false )
+#else
+#  define BENCHMARK( ... ) if ( false ) // NOLINT(readability-simplify-boolean-expr)
+#  define BENCHMARK_ADVANCED( name ) if ( false )
+#endif
+
+#endif // !CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_BENCHMARKS
+
 #endif // CATCH_BENCHMARK_HPP_INCLUDED
diff --git a/src/catch2/internal/catch_compiler_capabilities.hpp b/src/catch2/internal/catch_compiler_capabilities.hpp
index aa3af50..1ff3252 100644
--- a/src/catch2/internal/catch_compiler_capabilities.hpp
+++ b/src/catch2/internal/catch_compiler_capabilities.hpp
@@ -14,6 +14,7 @@
 // CATCH_CONFIG_WINDOWS_SEH : is Windows SEH supported?
 // CATCH_CONFIG_POSIX_SIGNALS : are POSIX signals supported?
 // CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_EXCEPTIONS : Are exceptions enabled?
+// CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_BENCHMARKS : is the benchmarking subsystem disabled?
 // ****************
 // Note to maintainers: if new toggles are added please document them
 // in configuration.md, too
solve_notes: Added an `#if !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_BENCHMARKS)` guard immediately after the two unconditional includes in catch_benchmark.hpp, wrapping all benchmark implementation headers and macros. The `#else` branch provides `if(false)` no-op stubs for BENCHMARK/BENCHMARK_ADVANCED (and their CATCH_-prefixed variants when CATCH_CONFIG_PREFIX_ALL is set) so user code still compiles without the benchmark runtime. Added the corresponding documentation line to the options comment block in catch_compiler_capabilities.hpp.

search_sequence.result: Header has two unconditional includes (catch_user_config.hpp and catch_compiler_capabilities.hpp) followed by all benchmark implementation includes and macro definitions; surrounded by a CATCH_BENCHMARK_HPP_INCLUDED guard
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to understand the full file layout to decide where to place the #if guard

search_sequence.action: Read CATCH_CONFIG_PREFIX_ALL usage at line 130 of catch_benchmark.hpp
search_sequence.result: BENCHMARK and BENCHMARK_ADVANCED macros have two definitions: one plain (BENCHMARK...) and one with CATCH_ prefix when CATCH_CONFIG_PREFIX_ALL is defined
search_sequence.reasoning: The no-op stubs in the #else block must replicate this duality or user code using either prefix form will fail to compile

search_sequence.action: Read documentation comment block at line 14 of catch_compiler_capabilities.hpp
search_sequence.result: Existing flags listed as // CATCH_CONFIG_XXX : description lines; CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_EXCEPTIONS at line 16 follows the same pattern
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed the exact format to add the CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_BENCHMARKS documentation line consistently

key_decisions.decision: Place the #if guard after the two unconditional includes rather than at the very top of the file
alternatives: Separate new header for the disabled stubs
key_decisions.reasoning: The two unconditional includes (catch_user_config.hpp and catch_compiler_capabilities.hpp) must always be present so that CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_BENCHMARKS itself can be defined before being tested; guarding after them is the minimal change

key_decisions.decision: Use `if ( false )` stubs for the disabled macros
key_decisions.reasoning: if(false) ensures the benchmark body still parses as valid C++ (lambda argument to BENCHMARK is syntactically consumed) while being eliminated by the optimizer

exploration_log.aha_moment: Seeing that catch_user_config.hpp and catch_compiler_capabilities.hpp were already included unconditionally at the top confirmed they must stay outside any new guard — the new flag can only be checked after those two files are processed.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have checked how CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_EXCEPTIONS disables features in its own header as an additional model, but the pattern was clear enough from reading catch_benchmark.hpp alone.

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: BENCHMARK
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: edited: now conditionally compiled under #if !defined(CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_BENCHMARKS); no-op stub added in #else branch

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: BENCHMARK_ADVANCED
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: edited: same conditional guard added; no-op stub added in #else branch

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_EXCEPTIONS
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: read: documentation pattern at line 16 was the model for adding the CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_BENCHMARKS doc comment

thrash_preventing_defs.name: CATCH_CONFIG_PREFIX_ALL
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: the #if defined(CATCH_CONFIG_PREFIX_ALL) block at line 130 defines CATCH_BENCHMARK/CATCH_BENCHMARK_ADVANCED variants; without reading this an agent would forget to provide the prefixed stubs, leaving users of CATCH_CONFIG_PREFIX_ALL with compile errors

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient contains the two edited macros and the doc-comment model in catch_compiler_capabilities.hpp. thrash_preventing adds the CATCH_CONFIG_PREFIX_ALL conditional block — without seeing it upfront, an agent would write the #else stubs for only the unprefixed BENCHMARK macros, miss the CATCH_BENCHMARK variants, and then thrash searching for why CATCH_CONFIG_PREFIX_ALL users break.

queries.query_text: Allow users to exclude the benchmarking subsystem at compile time so that projects that only need assertions do not pay the code-size cost of the benchmark runtime
queries.justification: Rule compliance: uses domain concepts 'exclude benchmarking subsystem', 'compile time', 'code-size', 'benchmark runtime' — no symbol names. Pre-implementation: a developer requesting this feature would describe it in these terms.

Rule compliance: 'CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_BENCHMARKS' is the exact literal string added to the source. Pre-implementation: a developer searching for how to disable benchmarks or looking for the flag name would grep for this string.

Rule compliance: five exact symbol/macro names from the diff. Pre-implementation: a developer working on benchmark compilation guards would identify these macro names from the task description and repo exploration.

queries.query_text: imports of catch_benchmark.hpp in benchmark subsystem headers
queries.justification: Rule compliance: names concrete file 'catch_benchmark.hpp' and uses relationship word 'imports'. Pre-implementation: a developer adding a compilation flag to the benchmark header would want to know who includes it.

Rule compliance: two explicit file paths from the solution. Pre-implementation: a developer adding a compile-time benchmark flag would navigate to the benchmark header and the capabilities header where flags are documented.

queries.query_text: Add preprocessor guard around BENCHMARK and BENCHMARK_ADVANCED macros so defining CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_BENCHMARKS skips the benchmark runtime
queries.justification: Rule compliance: mixes domain concept 'preprocessor guard', 'skip benchmark runtime' with exact symbols 'BENCHMARK' and 'BENCHMARK_ADVANCED'. Pre-implementation: a developer who has identified the macros as the entry point would frame the task this way.

queries.query_text: BENCHMARK BENCHMARK_ADVANCED CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_BENCHMARKS in benchmark/catch_benchmark.hpp internal/catch_compiler_capabilities.hpp
queries.justification: Rule compliance: three macro names plus two file paths. Pre-implementation: a developer who has located the relevant files would combine symbol and path searches.

queries.query_text: Add CATCH_CONFIG_DISABLE_BENCHMARKS flag: wrap benchmark implementation in catch_benchmark.hpp with #if guard and provide BENCHMARK no-op stubs in #else, document flag in catch_compiler_capabilities.hpp
queries.justification: Rule compliance: includes symbols, file paths, and domain context. Pre-implementation: this is the query a developer would write when starting to implement a compile-time benchmark disable flag.

test_selection.test_query: Find tests that verify the behavior of BENCHMARK and BENCHMARK_ADVANCED macros in src/catch2/benchmark/catch_benchmark.hpp

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: Exercises the BENCHMARK macro code path through Benchmark constructor and prepare template, covering the lines in catch_benchmark.hpp that are now under the #if guard
